title: Using Sora Editor with TextMate 0.18.1
content:
Hello, welcome to this blog. Today, we’re going to learn how to use Sora Editor with TextMate in version 0.18.1.

Load Default Themes

try {
	FileProviderRegistry.getInstance().addFileProvider(new AssetsFileResolver(getAssets()));
    
    String[] themes = {"quietlight", "darcula"};
    var themeRegistry = ThemeRegistry.getInstance();
    
    int i = themes.length;
    for (int j = 0; j < i; ) {
    	String name = themes[j];
        var path = "textmate/" + name + ".json";
        themeRegistry.loadTheme(new ThemeModel(IThemeSource.fromInputStream(FileProviderRegistry.getInstance().tryGetInputStream(path), path, null), name));
        j++;

    }
} catch (Exception e) {
	e.printStackTrace();
}

Load Default Languages

GrammarRegistry.getInstance().loadGrammars("textmate/languages.json");

Load Default Languages Dynamically

In this case, we load Java language as an example.

GrammarDefinition grammarDefinition = new GrammarDefinition() {
    @Override
    public String getName() {
        return "java";
    }

    @Override
    public String getLanguageConfiguration() {
        return "textmate/java/language-configuration.json";
    }

    @Override
    public String getScopeName() {
        return "source.java";
    }

    @Override
    public IGrammarSource getGrammar() {
        return new IGrammarSource() {
            @Override
            public String getFilePath() {
                return "textmate/java/syntaxes/java.tmLanguage.json";
            }

            @Override
            public Reader getReader() throws IOException {
                return null;
            }
        };
    }
};

GrammarRegistry.getInstance().loadGrammar(grammarDefinition);
GrammarRegistry.getInstance().loadLanguageAndLanguageConfiguration(grammarDefinition);

Ensure TextMate Themes

try {
    var editorColorScheme = binding.editor.getColorScheme();
    if (!(editorColorScheme instanceof TextMateColorScheme)) {
        editorColorScheme = TextMateColorScheme.create(ThemeRegistry.getInstance());
        binding.editor.setColorScheme(editorColorScheme);
    }
} catch (Exception e) {
    e.printStackTrace();
}

Apply Languages

TextMateLanguage language;
var editorLanguage = binding.editor.getEditorLanguage();
if (editorLanguage instanceof TextMateLanguage) {
	language = (TextMateLanguage) editorLanguage;
    language.updateLanguage("source.java");
} else {
    language = TextMateLanguage.create("source.java", true);
}
binding.editor.setEditorLanguage(language);

Apply Themes (With DayNight theme detection)

Use ThemeRegistry.getInstance().setTheme() to apply themes.

int nightModeFlags = getResources().getConfiguration().uiMode & Configuration.UI_MODE_NIGHT_MASK;
switch (nightModeFlags) {
case Configuration.UI_MODE_NIGHT_YES:
    ThemeRegistry.getInstance().setTheme("darcula");
    break;

case Configuration.UI_MODE_NIGHT_NO:
    ThemeRegistry.getInstance().setTheme("quietlight");
    break;

case Configuration.UI_MODE_NIGHT_UNDEFINED:

    break;
}

Full source code

public class MainActivity extends AppCompatActivity {
    ActivityTestBinding binding;
    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        binding = ActivityTestBinding.inflate(getLayoutInflater());
        setContentView(binding.getRoot());

        loadDefaultThemes();
        loadDefaultLanguages();

        ensureTextmateTheme();

        applyThemes();
        applyLanguages();

    }

    private void loadDefaultThemes() {

        try {
            FileProviderRegistry.getInstance().addFileProvider(new AssetsFileResolver(getAssets()));

            String[] themes = {
                "quietlight",
                "darcula"
            };
            var themeRegistry = ThemeRegistry.getInstance();

            int i = themes.length;
            for (int j = 0; j < i;) {
                String name = themes[j];
                var path = "textmate/" + name + ".json";
                themeRegistry.loadTheme(new ThemeModel(IThemeSource.fromInputStream(FileProviderRegistry.getInstance().tryGetInputStream(path), path, null), name));
                j++;

            }
        } catch (Exception e) {
            e.printStackTrace();
        }

    }

    public void loadDefaultLanguages() {
        GrammarRegistry.getInstance().loadGrammars("textmate/languages.json");
    }

    public void ensureTextmateTheme() {
        try {
            var editorColorScheme = binding.editor.getColorScheme();
            if (!(editorColorScheme instanceof TextMateColorScheme)) {
                editorColorScheme = TextMateColorScheme.create(ThemeRegistry.getInstance());
                binding.editor.setColorScheme(editorColorScheme);
            }
        } catch (Exception e) {
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }

    public void applyThemes() {

        int nightModeFlags = getResources().getConfiguration().uiMode & Configuration.UI_MODE_NIGHT_MASK;
        switch (nightModeFlags) {
        case Configuration.UI_MODE_NIGHT_YES:
            ThemeRegistry.getInstance().setTheme("darcula");
            break;

        case Configuration.UI_MODE_NIGHT_NO:
            ThemeRegistry.getInstance().setTheme("quietlight");
            break;
        }

    }

    public void applyLanguages() {

        ensureTextmateTheme();

        TextMateLanguage language;
        var editorLanguage = binding.editor.getEditorLanguage();
        if (editorLanguage instanceof TextMateLanguage) {
            language = (TextMateLanguage) editorLanguage;
            language.updateLanguage("source.java");
        } else {
            language = TextMateLanguage.create("source.java", true);
        }
        binding.editor.setEditorLanguage(language);

    }
}

That’s all. I hope this blog post would help you.
